behavior_chunks.label: hand_biting
behavior_chunks.evidence: A child sitting in a chair bites their own hand while two adults are present in the room.
behavior_chunks.justification: The child is seen bringing their hand to their mouth and biting it while the adult in the teal shirt leans in.

behavior_chunks.label: head_banging
behavior_chunks.evidence: A child in a brown jacket is sitting between two adults; around the 1-second mark, the child raises their hand and repeatedly hits their own forehead while an adult attempts to intervene.
behavior_chunks.justification: The child is seen repeatedly striking their own head with their hand, which fits the definition of head_banging.

behavior_chunks.label: hand_biting
behavior_chunks.evidence: A woman in a grey shirt is leaning over and holding a child's arm, preventing the child from bringing their hand to their mouth.
behavior_chunks.justification: The child is attempting to bite their own hand, and the adult is intervening to stop the behavior.

behavior_chunks.label: hitting_others
behavior_chunks.evidence: The child patient is sitting in a blue chair and begins to hit the thigh of the person sitting next to them starting at 0:01 and continuing until approximately 0:02.
behavior_chunks.justification: The child is observed repeatedly hitting another person's leg, which fits the definition of hitting_others.

behavior_chunks.label: hand_biting
behavior_chunks.evidence: The child patient sits between two adults and begins to bite their own hand starting around 2 seconds into the video.
behavior_chunks.justification: The child puts their hand/fingers in their mouth and bites down on them.

behavior_chunks.label: hand_biting
behavior_chunks.evidence: The child is sitting between two adults and brings their hand up to their mouth to bite it.
behavior_chunks.justification: The child clearly places their hand in their mouth and bites it for approximately one second.

behavior_chunks.label: scratching
behavior_chunks.evidence: A child enters the room and begins scratching their head/scalp area while standing next to an adult.
